articles.title: Israeli security officials say shooting suspect is Palestinian citizen of Israel
articles.summary: Israeli security officials say shooting suspect is Palestinian citizen of Israel Israeli security officials have said that the suspect in a deadly shooting in central Israel is a resident of Tayibe, a city populated predominantly by Palestinian citizens of Israel, north of the scene of the attack. Images of the scene appear to show an abandoned submachine gun. Israeli forces and the police confirmed that the suspect was "neutralised", while a manhunt is underway for a potential second shooter.

articles.title: Ben Gvir calls for execution of shooter suspects
articles.summary: Ben Gvir calls for execution of shooter suspects Israeli far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir has called for the execution of the suspects in a deadly shooting attack in central Israel on Sunday. Israeli police said they had "neutralised" one suspect and are currently searching for the second. “If the terrorist is caught alive he will be executed. This is the law and we will demand its implementation,” Ben Gvir said in a post on X. “Jewish blood is not in vain. Whoever murders a Jew will see the hanging rope.”

articles.title: One killed, five wounded in shooting attack in Israel
articles.summary: One killed, five wounded in shooting attack in Israel One person has been killed and five others wounded in a series of shooting attacks in central Israel, an Israeli police spokesperson has confirmed. The shootings reportedly struck civilians at several locations near the entrance to Kochav Yair. The police said that one suspect was "neutralised" and are currently searching for another. The Israeli military says it has deployed troops to the area, while the Israeli Home Front Command has instructed residents of the Tzur Yitzhak settlement to remain indoors until further notice. Israel claims killing of Hamas commander in southern Gaza
articles.summary: Israel claims killing of Hamas commander in southern Gaza The Israeli army says that its forces killed a Hamas commander allegedly among those who participated in the attack on southern Israel on 7 October. In a post on X, the military said it struck and killed Sakr Abu Karim in southern Gaza, a commander in Hamas's Nukhba Force who they say helmed the raid in the Kissufim area on October 7, 2023. It added that another Hamas member who served as a “communications operative” was also killed in the attack.

articles.title: Israeli forces continue to pummel southern Lebanon
articles.summary: Israeli forces continue to pummel southern Lebanon Israeli attacks have continued across southern Lebanon overnight and into Sunday, Lebanon's National News Agency (NNA) reported. The towns of al-Qatrani, Byblos and Rihan in the Jezzine district were struck overnight. In the Tyre district, Deir Kifa, Barashit and Chaqra were also targeted. On Sunday, NNA reported that two people were wounded when an "anti-government" drone struck Shahabiyeh. Attacks were also reported on the town of Srifa in Tyre, and Dweir, near Nabatieh, north of the Litani River. According to the delegation, the attacks threaten the global nuclear non-proliferation regime and undermine the principles of the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) and the IAEA safeguards system. articles.title: UN peacekeepers say strike violated Lebanon’s sovereignty
articles.summary: UN peacekeepers say strike violated Lebanon’s sovereignty The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) expressed “heartfelt condolences” to the families of the Lebanese army personnel killed in an Israeli attack in the Nabatieh area of southern Lebanon. In a post on X, Unifil said such attacks on Lebanese territory constitute “gross violations” of Lebanon’s sovereignty, territorial integrity and UN Security Council Resolution 1701. In an interview with Al Jazeera, Al Moussawi questioned why a ceasefire would not extend to southern Lebanon, calling instead for a complete halt to hostilities without conditions. articles.title: Yemen condemns Iranian attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait
articles.summary: Yemen condemns Iranian attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait Yemen’s foreign ministry condemned Iran’s recent attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait, describing them as a dangerous escalation and accusing Tehran of threatening regional security and stability. In a statement, the ministry said the attacks exposed what it called the “aggressive and terrorist nature” of the Iranian government. Yemen joined several other Arab countries, including Egypt, Qatar, Palestine and Saudi Arabia, in condemning the strikes. articles.title: Video: Senior Iranian official rules out Trump-Khamenei meeting
articles.summary: Video: Senior Iranian official rules out Trump-Khamenei meeting Mohsen Rezaei, a senior military adviser to Iran’s supreme leader, said negotiations with the United States are currently at a standstill and ruled out the possibility of a meeting between US President Donald Trump and Mojtaba Khamenei, according to an interview with CNN. Rezaei said Washington must release $24 billion in frozen Iranian assets as a “test of trust” to move negotiations forward, arguing that the funds belong to Iran. articles.summary: Hezbollah claims drone and rocket attacks on Israeli forces in south Lebanon Hezbollah said it carried out a series of attacks against Israeli forces in southern Lebanon, including a strike on an Israeli army command headquarters in Naqoura using an Ababil attack drone. In separate statements, the group said it also targeted Israeli soldiers on the southern outskirts of Haddatha with two drones and attacked a gathering of Israeli troops and military vehicles near Yahmar al-Shaqif using rocket barrages and artillery fire.
